messages.content: write a code in c++ which get URL argument as string and should return dictionary where key is URL parameter name and value is URL parameter value
how can i get value by key from std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>
how can i check if std::unordered_map contains an element in c++ 11

why im getting error "no template named 'unordered_map' in namespace 'std'; did you mean 'unordered_set'?"

when i try build apk file in unreal engine

The error message you're encountering suggests that the `std::unordered_map` header may not be accessible or supported in your current environment. This could be due to a few reasons:

1. Compiler version: Certain older compilers may not fully support the C++11 standard, including the `std::unordered_map` library. Ensure that you are using a C++11 or newer compliant compiler.

2. Unreal Engine specific issue: Unreal Engine may have its own header or library that provides similar functionality to `std::unordered_map`. Check the Unreal Engine documentation or forums to ensure you are utilizing the correct classes or headers.

3. Include directives: Verify that you have included the necessary header file at the beginning of your code. In this case, ensure you have `#include <unordered_map>` before attempting to use `std::unordered_map`.

If none of these suggestions resolves the issue, it may be beneficial to consult the Unreal Engine documentation or forums for more specific guidance on working with containers and libraries in Unreal Engine.
